note: Mathlib/Analysis/Convex/Segment.lean:51 -- def segment (x y : E) : Set E := {z | ∃ a b, 0≤a ∧ 0≤b ∧ a+b=1 ∧ a•x+b•y=z}, docstring 'Segments in a vector space'; this is the closed line segment joining two points, defined as the convex-combination set (matches ℝ, ℝⁿ, etc.), the standard characterization of the geometric line segment.
verify_note: decl confirmed at Mathlib/Analysis/Convex/Segment.lean:51 with exact quoted body/docstring; QID166154='line segment' (Wikidata); segment IS the standard closed-line-segment definition (convex-combination set), so 'exact' is honest despite generalizing to arbitrary 𝕜-vector spaces.

note: Mathlib/Algebra/IsPrimePow.lean:27 -- def IsPrimePow : Prop := ∃ (p : R) (k : ℕ), Prime p ∧ 0 < k ∧ p ^ k = n, docstring 'n is a prime power if there is a prime p and a positive natural k such that n can be written as p^k' -- exact formalization of the prime-power concept.
verify_note: decl confirmed at Mathlib/Algebra/IsPrimePow.lean:27, docstring/body quoted verbatim matches note; QID1667469='prime power' (Wikidata: positive integer power of a prime); IsPrimePow IS the direct formalization, 'exact' is honest.
